activities_projects.title: SAS Triangle Construction
activities_projects.objective: Construct a triangle using Side-Angle-Side criteria
steps: Draw a base line $BC = 6$ cm
At point $B$, construct an angle of $70^{\circ}$ using a protractor
Use a compass to mark a point $A$ on the $70^{\circ}$ ray such that $AB = 5$ cm
Join $A$ to $C$ and measure the third side $AC$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Accurately constructs a triangle when two sides and one included angle are known
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of the $70^{\circ}$ angle and side lengths (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: ASA Triangle Construction
activities_projects.objective: Construct a triangle using Angle-Side-Angle criteria
steps: Draw a base line $XY = 7$ cm
At point $X$, construct $\angle YXZ = 45^{\circ}$
At point $Y$, construct $\angle XYZ = 60^{\circ}$
Extend the rays until they intersect at point $Z$ to complete the triangle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the construction of triangles using base angles
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct intersection point and angle accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 90-Degree Compass Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Construct a perpendicular line without a protractor
steps: Draw a line $L$ and mark a point $P$ on it
Construct a $60^{\circ}$ and a $120^{\circ}$ arc from point $P$
Bisect the angle between $60^{\circ}$ and $120^{\circ}$ to find the $90^{\circ}$ line
Verify the perpendicularity using the corner of a notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses bisecting techniques to create right angles
activities_projects.evaluation: Success in creating a perfect $90^{\circ}$ angle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Semi-Regular Tilings (Two Shapes)
activities_projects.objective: Combine different polygons to tile a surface
materials: Cutouts of squares and equilateral triangles (same side length)
steps: Arrange the squares and triangles so that they meet at every vertex in the same pattern
Try a pattern of one square surrounded by triangles
Ensure no gaps or overlaps exist
Trace the pattern into your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores the transition from regular to semi-regular tessellations
activities_projects.evaluation: Consistency of the vertex pattern (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: RHS Triangle Construction
activities_projects.objective: Construct a right-angled triangle using the RHS rule
steps: Draw a base of 4 cm
Construct a $90^{\circ}$ angle at one endpoint
Set the compass to 5 cm (hypotenuse) and draw an arc from the other endpoint to cut the perpendicular line
Complete the triangle and measure the third side
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies RHS criteria to geometric construction
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct placement of the hypotenuse arc (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Angle Bisector Network
activities_projects.objective: Construct the Incenter of a triangle
steps: Construct any large scalene triangle
Construct the angle bisectors for all three angles
Identify the point where all three bisectors meet (Incenter)
Try to draw a circle inside the triangle using this point as the center
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers the point of concurrency for angle bisectors
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of the meeting point of the three lines (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tiling with Isosceles Triangles
steps: Cut 12 identical isosceles triangles
Experiment with 'Base-to-Base' and 'Side-to-Side' arrangements
Determine which arrangement fills the space more effectively
Glue the best tiling onto a sheet
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that symmetry helps in tiling irregular shapes

activities_projects.title: Constructing a Parallel Transversal
activities_projects.objective: Create a specific angle on a parallel line
steps: Draw two parallel lines $l$ and $m$
Draw a transversal line $t$
If the angle at $l$ is $55^{\circ}$, use a compass to copy that exact angle onto line $m$ at the corresponding position
Verify using a protractor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects angle copying skills with parallel line properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the copied angle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Quadrilateral Tiler
activities_projects.objective: Prove that any quadrilateral can tile a plane
steps: Cut out 4 identical irregular quadrilaterals
Rotate them so that all four different angles meet at one central point
Observe that the sum is $360^{\circ}$ and they fit perfectly
Repeat to extend the pattern
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the $360^{\circ}$ vertex property of tilings
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct orientation of shapes to eliminate gaps (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Perpendicular from an External Point
activities_projects.objective: Drop a perpendicular from a point not on the line
steps: Draw a line $XY$ and a point $A$ above it
Swing an arc from $A$ that cuts $XY$ at two points, $P$ and $Q$
From $P$ and $Q$, draw two intersecting arcs below the line
Join $A$ to the intersection to create the perpendicular
